intent: Close out the remaining local residue after the input-model clean-break migration by removing only helper-classified safe residue, preserving unrelated or ambiguous work, and recording an auditable disposition without opening a PR.

scope.summary: Residue disposition receipt for the dirty original worktree and a clean receipt-only branch-no-pr closeout branch.

profile_selection_receipt.selection_rationale: The operator explicitly required branch-no-pr and prohibited PR creation. The cleanup is a bounded atomic closeout/disposition pass; no hard gate requires transitional coexistence.

dispositions.evidence: git diff --name-status origin/main -- $(git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD) returned no differences; the remaining local branch commit is patch-equivalent to landed origin/main history.

.octon/inputs/exploratory/runtime-adapters-experimental-external.yml is absent from the working tree and absent from origin/main; git add -u staged the local deletion relative to the obsolete branch index.

dispositions.group: local run, publication, prompt-alignment, generated scratch, control, continuity, and evidence residue
dispositions.evidence: cleanup-local-run-artifacts.sh dry-run classified 814 untracked files as cleanup candidates, 47 protected referenced files, and 83 manual-review files; confirm mode removed only the 814 cleanup candidates; follow-up dry-run reported zero cleanup candidates.
Broad staged and unstaged exploratory moves remain logically separate from the input clean-break closeout and require their own validation if pursued.
Skill and host projection changes are unrelated to the residue cleanup and remain preserved.

dispositions.group: Rust, audit, plan, and process-incoming-intake skill assets
dispositions.evidence: Untracked skill assets are coherent but unrelated; no safe deletion or landing authority was proven.
Release/version changes are separate release hygiene work and were not mixed into this cleanup.

dispositions.group: generated/effective outputs and state/control/evidence files not classified as cleanup candidates
dispositions.evidence: The cleanup helper protected referenced files and left manual-review artifacts for explicit future classification.

landing_evaluation.blocker_reason: Hosted landing evidence is produced after this receipt commit exists; final response records the resulting landing, sync, and cleanup evidence.

not_landed_reason: This receipt is recorded before hosted no-PR landing preflight and landing are run.
not_cleaned_reason: The original dirty branch retains ambiguous user-owned work and cannot be deleted; cleanup can only claim removal of helper-classified safe residue plus preservation of unrelated groups.
cleanup_evidence_refs: git add -u .octon/inputs/exploratory/runtime-adapters-experimental-external.yml aligned the obsolete branch index with origin/main for the retired file
cleanup-local-run-artifacts.sh --summary-only after cleanup: cleanup_candidates=0, protected_referenced=47, manual_review=83

source_branch_cleanup.blocker_reason: Source branch cleanup must wait until hosted landing and origin/main containment are proven.
evidence_refs: git-worktree-autonomy-contract requires containment before branch cleanup

review_waiver_refs: operator explicitly requested branch-no-pr and prohibited PR creation

rollback_handle.instructions: Before hosted landing, delete the local and remote source branch. After hosted landing, revert the landed receipt commit if the audit record itself must be removed; the local cleanup removed only untracked helper-classified cleanup candidates.

remaining_blockers: Original branch chore/inputs-clean-break is active and dirty; do not delete it while ambiguous user-owned work remains.

Exploratory moves, synthesize-research changes, Rust/intake skill assets, release/version files, generated/effective outputs, and manual-review evidence require separate classification before mutation.
This receipt cannot self-reference a post-commit hosted landed SHA; final closeout response records hosted landing evidence separately if landing succeeds.
